title: NTM Fluid Converters
description: NTM Fluid Converters bridges Hbm's Nuclear Tech Mod fluids with Forge fluid handling. Fully compatible with both the original HBM NTM and NTM Space fork
body:
# NTM Fluid Converters - HBM NTM Addon NTM Fluid Converters is a small addon for [HBM's Nuclear Tech Mod](https://github.com/HbmMods/Hbm-s-Nuclear-Tech-GIT) that adds two blocks for bridging HBM's internal fluid system with the standard Forge fluid system. This lets you move HBM fluids in and out of any Forge-compatible tank, pipe, or machine. Compatible with both the original HBM NTM and [JameH2's space fork](https://github.com/JameH2/Hbm-s-Nuclear-Tech-GIT). # Features - **HBM to Forge Converter**: Accepts fluid from HBM pipes and outputs it into any Forge `IFluidHandler` compatible block - **Forge to HBM Converter**: Accepts fluid from any Forge `IFluidHandler` source and outputs it into HBM's fluid network # FAQ **Is this the official NTM?**
No. This is an addon for NTM which works both with the original and space fork. **Can I use this for my Modpack?**
Absolutely! **Does it work with NTM version X?**
Yes. It always works with the latest NTM version. You do not need to update NTM Fluid Converters when NTM updates, unless the NTM mod author changes the internal fluid logic in a way that breaks this mod. **Why is the converter not fast enough?**
This is due to an internal tank limit of 8,000 mB. To increase the throughput you can edit the config file. # Issues & Support Found a bug or have a feature request? Open an issue on [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Justus0405/NTM-Fluid-Converters/issues). For questions and discussion, join the [Discord server](https://discord.justus0405.com/). # Contributing & Building Check out our [build guide](https://github.com/Justus0405/NTM-Fluid-Converters/blob/main/docs/BUILD_GUIDE.md)! # Licensing - **Code**: This mod's code is licensed under the MIT License. See [LICENSE_CODE](https://github.com/Justus0405/NTM-Fluid-Converters/blob/main/LICENSE_CODE). - **Assets**: The assets are licensed under the Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International License. See [LICENSE_ASSETS](https://github.com/Justus0405/NTM-Fluid-Converters/blob/main/LICENSE_ASSETS). #
